summary: Cleaned up sysdolphin/baselib unknown-file organization by deleting several placeholder/unknown assembly and header files, renaming the hsd_3915 translation unit to particle, moving shared BSS/SBSS ownership into hsd_3A94, and replacing the catch-all __baselib.h declarations with more targeted headers. The PR was a structural cleanup rather than a commented decomp review; there were no PR body notes or review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Renamed the hsd_3915 assembly translation unit to particle.s, including changing the embedded .file directive from "hsd_3915.c" to "particle.c".
Renamed build references from sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3915.c or hsd_3915.s.o to sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c or particle.s.o in configure.py and obj_files.mk.
Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt to use sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c for the 0x80391580-0x8039F78C text range.
Moved the BSS and SBSS ranges previously represented as baselib_shared_data_001.c and baselib_shared_data_002.c into the sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.c split entry.
Appended BSS/SBSS symbol reservations for hsd_804D1138 through hsd_804D2E70 and hsd_804D7980 through hsd_804D79C8 to asm/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.s.
Deleted the catch-all src/sysdolphin/baselib/__baselib.h and redistributed declarations into targeted headers such as hsd_3A94.h, particle.h, and psdisptev.h.

Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.h with placeholder structs and extern declarations for hsd_804D0B50, hsd_804D0C54, hsd_804D1138, hsd_804D1148, hsd_804D2348, hsd_804D2648, hsd_804D2E70, hsd_804D79A0, hsd_804D79A4, and hsd_804D79A8.

Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.h with prototypes for hsd_80392E80, hsd_803931A4, hsd_80393A04, hsd_80393A54, hsd_8039D4DC, hsd_8039D5DC, hsd_8039EFAC, hsd_8039F6CC and externs for several hsd_804D78xx globals.
Updated includes in gmmain.c and grlib.c from <baselib/hsd_3915.h> to <baselib/particle.h>.
Updated lbcollision.c to include <baselib/particle.h> instead of <baselib/baselib_shared_data_003.h>.
Updated hsd_3B33.c to include "hsd_3A94.h" instead of "__baselib.h".
Added psSetupTevCommon, psSetupTevInvalidState, and psSetupTev prototypes to psdisptev.h.
Expanded tools/functions.sh melee_sed coverage to include config/GALE01/splits.txt, config/GALE01/symbols.txt, and configure.py, and updated rename_tu to handle .dox files.
smart_moves: Avoided keeping anonymous shared-data translation units around by assigning their BSS/SBSS ranges to a real neighboring baselib TU, hsd_3A94.
Renamed a large unknown hsd_3915 unit to the more meaningful particle name and propagated the rename through source includes, assembly, splits, configure.py, and obj_files.mk.
Replaced the broad private __baselib.h umbrella with narrower declarations in subsystem-specific headers, reducing accidental coupling.
Kept address comments on newly added header declarations, making unresolved globals and functions searchable by address while their final names remain unknown.
Updated tooling so future scripted renames cover build configuration and .dox documentation files, not only src/asm/docs C/S/H paths.
decomp_lessons: When an unknown translation unit gets a better subsystem name, update every layer together: asm .file directive, src/header filenames, includes, splits.txt, configure.py, obj_files.mk, and documentation stubs.
Empty or placeholder shared-data files are not always useful long term; if a BSS/SBSS range clearly belongs with a concrete TU, moving the split ownership can simplify the object list.
Prefer targeted baselib headers over a catch-all private header once declarations can be grouped by the code that actually owns them.
For still-unknown data, byte-array placeholder structs and address-commented externs are acceptable cleanup scaffolding as long as the sizes and split ranges preserve matching.
Rename tooling should include generated/build metadata such as splits.txt, symbols.txt, and configure.py, because missing those files leaves stale TU names after a source rename.
naming_conventions: The former hsd_3915 unit was renamed to particle, yielding particle.s, particle.c, particle.h, and particle.dox.
Header include guards in new headers use GALE01-address style names such as GALE01_391580 and GALE01_3A949C.
Unknown global/function declarations retain hsd_80xxxxxx or hsd_803xxxxx names with leading address comments such as /* 392E80 */ and /* 4D78D0 */.
Temporary unknown structs in hsd_3A94.h use __baselib_UnkType### names with explicit byte-array sizes.
Includes use the public baselib path form in game code, e.g. <baselib/particle.h>.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved raw .bss and .sbss .skip reservations from separate baselib_shared_data assembly files into hsd_3A94.s to preserve addresses while removing extra object files.
Updated splits.txt so hsd_3A94.c owns its .data plus the moved .bss and .sbss ranges.
Preserved the existing nonmatching object status in configure.py while changing only the TU name from hsd_3915.c to particle.c.
Removed standalone unknown assembly files rather than trying to decompile or rename the functions in this PR; the evidence points to organization cleanup, not new matching work.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body was null, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred only from changed files.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for particle.h or particle.c to see whether hsd_80392E80, hsd_803931A4, hsd_80393A04, and related particle functions received real names.
Search later PRs for hsd_3A94.h and hsd_804D1138 to see whether the placeholder __baselib_UnkType structs were refined.
Check whether tools/functions.sh rename_tu was further changed after adding .dox support and config file coverage.
Look for PRs that decompile or rename psdisptev functions after prototypes were moved out of __baselib.h.
